mission.title: Install and Configure MicroK8s
mission.description: MicroK8s is a lightweight, single-package Kubernetes distribution designed for developers and edge computing. It simplifies Kubernetes installation and management, making it ideal for local development environments and CI/CD pipelines. While it offers a minimal footprint, users should be aware of its limitations in high-availability scenarios.

steps.title: Install MicroK8s
steps.description:
This step installs MicroK8s using the Snap package manager, which is the recommended method for installation.

```bash

sudo snap install microk8s --classic

```

> Installs the latest stable version of MicroK8s.

steps.title: Add User to MicroK8s Group
steps.description:
This step allows your user to run MicroK8s commands without sudo by adding it to the microk8s group.

```bash

sudo usermod -a -G microk8s $USER

```

> Replace $USER with your username if not running in a shell.

steps.title: Verify MicroK8s Installation
steps.description:
Check the status of MicroK8s to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

microk8s status --wait-ready

```

> Waits until MicroK8s is fully initialized.

steps.title: Enable Add-ons
steps.description:
This step enables essential add-ons like DNS and the Kubernetes dashboard for better functionality.

```bash

microk8s enable dns

microk8s enable dashboard

```

> Enables the DNS add-on.
> Enables the Kubernetes dashboard.

steps.title: Verify Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the nodes in your MicroK8s cluster to ensure everything is operational.

```bash

microk8s kubectl get nodes

```

> Should show the local node in a Ready state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall MicroK8s
uninstall.description:
This step removes MicroK8s from your system. Be aware that this will delete all Kubernetes resources and configurations.

```bash

sudo snap remove microk8s

```

> This command will remove MicroK8s and all associated data.

uninstall.title: Remove User from MicroK8s Group
uninstall.description:
This step removes your user from the microk8s group to prevent future access.

```bash

sudo gpasswd -d $USER microk8s

```

> Replace $USER with your username if not running in a shell.

upgrade.title: Upgrade MicroK8s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configurations and resources. This step will upgrade MicroK8s to the latest version.

```bash

microk8s stop

sudo snap refresh microk8s

microk8s start

microk8s kubectl get nodes

```

> Stop MicroK8s before upgrading.
> Upgrades MicroK8s to the latest version.
> Start MicroK8s after the upgrade.
> Verify that nodes are in a Ready state post-upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'microk8s is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s may not have started properly after installation.

**Fix:**
Run 'microk8s start' to start the service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl command is not linked to the MicroK8s installation.

**Fix:**
Use 'microk8s kubectl' instead of 'kubectl'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s may not be fully initialized or running.

**Fix:**
Check the status with 'microk8s status --wait-ready'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The host machine may not meet the minimum resource requirements.

**Fix:**
Ensure the machine has at least 2 vCPU and 4GB RAM.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31 features, including improved scheduling and resource management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy add-ons that are no longer maintained.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME           STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION' when running 'microk8s kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a compatible Linux distribution and the Snap package manager installed.
